description_long: This object is a vintage-style portable radio receiver with a rectangular body featuring a wood-textured upper section and a metallic lower section. The front face displays a woven speaker grille above a control panel with a frequency indicator, two round dials, and two illuminated indicator lights. A sturdy, dark-colored handle arches over the top, providing easy portability, while a thin, metallic antenna extends vertically from one corner. The back is plain wood with a metallic access panel, likely for battery or maintenance access. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining functional controls with a classic aesthetic.
description: A vintage portable radio receiver with a wood and metal body, front speaker grille, control panel, handle, and antenna.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wood-textured body, dark handle, and metallic base, with the antenna barely visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the woven speaker grille, control panel with dials, frequency indicator, and indicator lights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the antenna clearly visible, showing the same wood and metal construction as the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the plain wood back and a metallic access panel, with the antenna extending upward.
